title: Toasted Crunchy Australian Seeds Skewers
description: A crispy and indulgent Australian dish featuring toasted seeds, cooked to perfection on a binchotan grill, with a tagine-inspired flavor profile, this recipe is raw-friendly and perfect for a snack or light meal.
instructions: Preheat the binchotan grill to medium-high heat, around 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the pumpkin seeds, sunflower seeds, and sesame seeds.
In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, lemon juice, smoked paprika, garlic powder, and salt.
Pour the wet ingredients over the seed mixture and stir until the seeds are evenly coated.
Thread the seed mixture onto skewers, leaving a small space between each seed.
Place the skewers on the preheated grill and cook for 5-7 minutes, or until the seeds are toasted and crispy.
Flip the skewers halfway through the cooking time to ensure even toasting.
Remove the skewers from the grill and let them cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the toasted seed skewers hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
